inputs.scenario: Pod 'api-server-7d8f9c-xkp2v' in namespace 'production' is in CrashLoopBackOff. Restart count: 18. Container exits with code 1 within seconds of starting.

expected_actions: Describe the pod to read container exit message and events
Fetch recent logs (tail_lines=50) to find the startup error
Fix root cause: bad env var, missing config, or application bug
If caused by missing Secret/ConfigMap, create it first
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: api-server-7d8f9c-xkp2v is CrashLoopBackOff (18 restarts). Pulled logs — container exits with 'Error: DATABASE_URL not set'. The pod references a secret key that does not exist. Recommended action: verify Secret 'api-server-secrets' exists and contains DATABASE_URL, then rolling-restart the deployment.

inputs.scenario: Deployment 'worker' in namespace 'jobs' has 0/3 pods ready. All pods show CrashLoopBackOff with exit code 137.

expected_actions: Exit code 137 = OOMKilled (SIGKILL). Confirm via lastState.terminated.reason.
Check current memory limit on the deployment
Increase memory limit via kubectl_patch_resource_limits
Monitor after change to confirm pods stabilise
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: worker pods are OOMKilled (exit 137). Current memory limit is 256Mi but container needs more. Recommended action: increase memory limit to 512Mi and request to 256Mi using kubectl_patch_resource_limits on the 'worker' container.

inputs.scenario: Pod 'ml-inference-6b7d9f-abc12' in namespace 'ml' was OOMKilled. Memory limit is 512Mi. Pod restarts every 10–15 minutes.

expected_actions: Check kubectl_top_pods to see current memory consumption
Compare actual usage to current limit (512Mi)
Increase memory limit — suggested 1Gi limit / 512Mi request
Optionally profile the application to find memory leaks
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: ml-inference is hitting its 512Mi memory limit. kubectl top shows peak usage of ~490Mi. Recommended action: raise memory limit to 1Gi and request to 512Mi to prevent OOMKill. If usage keeps growing, investigate application for memory leaks.

inputs.scenario: New deployment 'frontend-v2' in namespace 'web' is stuck. Pods show ImagePullBackOff. Image tag is 'acr.example.com/frontend:v2.3.1'.

expected_actions: Describe the pod — Events section shows exact pull error
Verify the image tag exists in the registry
Check imagePullSecrets — ensure the pull secret is present and valid
If tag is wrong, patch the deployment with the correct image
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: frontend-v2 pods cannot pull image 'acr.example.com/frontend:v2.3.1'. Event: '401 Unauthorized' — imagePullSecret is missing or expired. Recommended action: verify Secret 'acr-pull-secret' exists in namespace 'web' and is referenced in the deployment's imagePullSecrets.

inputs.scenario: Deployment 'data-processor' in namespace 'etl' has been Pending for 20 minutes. Requested: cpu=4000m, memory=8Gi. Cluster has 3 nodes.

expected_actions: Describe the pod — Events show 'Insufficient cpu' or 'Insufficient memory'
Run kubectl_top_nodes to see current node utilisation
Check node capacity vs. requested resources
Options: reduce resource requests, add a node, or scale down other workloads
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: data-processor pods are Pending due to insufficient resources. Each pod requests 4 CPU / 8Gi RAM but no single node has that free. Recommended action: reduce CPU request to 2000m and memory to 4Gi if possible, or add a larger node to the cluster.

inputs.scenario: StatefulSet 'postgres' in namespace 'data' has 1/1 pods Pending for 5 minutes. Event: '0/3 nodes are available: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims'.

expected_actions: List PVCs — find the one in Pending state
Describe the PVC to see provisioner error
Common causes: no StorageClass, storage quota exceeded, CSI driver error
Fix the StorageClass or manually provision a PV
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: postgres StatefulSet pod is Pending because PVC 'data-postgres-0' cannot be bound. No StorageClass named 'fast-ssd' exists in the cluster. Recommended action: create the StorageClass or update the StatefulSet to use an available StorageClass (e.g. 'gp2').

inputs.scenario: Deployment 'checkout-service' in namespace 'ecommerce' is stuck at 1/3 ready after an image update 10 minutes ago. New pods start but never become Ready.

expected_actions: Describe a new pod — look for readiness probe failures in Events
Check logs for startup errors or wrong port binding
Verify readiness probe path and port match what the app actually serves
If the new image is broken, rollback via kubectl_rollback_deployment
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: checkout-service rollout is stalled — new pods fail readiness. Events show: 'Readiness probe failed: GET /health 404'. The new image serves health check at /healthz not /health. Recommended action: either rollback to the previous revision or patch the deployment's readinessProbe.httpGet.path to /healthz.

inputs.scenario: HPA 'api-gateway' in namespace 'prod' has been at max replicas (20/20) for 30 minutes. CPU utilisation is at 95%.

expected_actions: Confirm HPA is at maxReplicas and CPU is still above target
Check if traffic spike is legitimate or caused by a bug/loop
Short term: increase maxReplicas via kubectl_patch_hpa
Long term: optimise the application or right-size CPU requests
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: api-gateway HPA is saturated at 20/20 replicas with 95% CPU. Pods cannot scale further. Recommended action: increase maxReplicas to 40 as a temporary measure, then investigate the CPU spike — check for hot endpoints or missing caching.

inputs.scenario: Node 'ip-10-0-1-42.ec2.internal' shows NotReady for 8 minutes. 3 deployments have pods on this node.

expected_actions: Describe the node — check conditions (DiskPressure, MemoryPressure, NetworkUnavailable)
Check recent events for kubelet errors
Cordon the node to prevent new scheduling while investigating
If node is unrecoverable, drain it so pods reschedule elsewhere
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: Node ip-10-0-1-42 is NotReady. Condition: DiskPressure=True — kubelet is evicting pods due to disk full. Recommended action: (1) cordon the node immediately, (2) investigate disk usage (likely large log files or image layers), (3) if unrecoverable, drain the node so workloads reschedule onto healthy nodes.

inputs.scenario: Service 'payment-svc' in namespace 'billing' has 0 endpoints. The backend deployment 'payment-api' has 3 running pods.

expected_actions: Describe the service — check selector labels
List pods with their labels — confirm they match the service selector
Check the service's targetPort matches the containerPort in the pod spec
Fix: patch the service selector or add missing labels to the deployment
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: payment-svc has no endpoints. Service selector is 'app=payment-api' but pods are labelled 'app=payment'. Recommended action: patch the deployment to add label 'app=payment-api' or update the Service selector to match 'app=payment'.

inputs.scenario: 10 pods across namespaces 'staging' and 'dev' show status Evicted. Eviction message: 'The node was low on resource: ephemeral-storage'.

expected_actions: Identify which node(s) have disk pressure
Clean up large log files, unused images (docker/crictl image prune)
Add ephemeral-storage limits to pods to prevent single pods from filling disk
Delete the Evicted pod objects — they don't restart automatically
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: 10 pods were Evicted due to low ephemeral-storage. Node 'ip-10-0-2-77' is at 92% disk usage. Recommended actions: (1) prune unused container images on that node, (2) delete the Evicted pod objects, (3) add ephemeral-storage limits to deployments to prevent recurrence.

inputs.scenario: Pod 'cache-warmer-xyz' in namespace 'app' has 47 restarts over 6 hours. Pod runs for ~5 minutes each time, then exits with code 0.

expected_actions: Exit code 0 = clean exit, not a crash — the process is finishing its work
Check if this is a Job being run as a Deployment by mistake
Or the container command finishes and the restart policy keeps reviving it
Fix: convert to a CronJob, set restartPolicy=Never, or keep the process running
outputs.expected_response: INFO: cache-warmer is not crashing — it exits cleanly (code 0) after ~5 min. The Deployment's restartPolicy=Always keeps restarting it. This is likely a batch job misdeployed as a Deployment. Recommended action: convert to a Kubernetes CronJob if it should run periodically, or adjust the container entrypoint to run as a long-lived process.

inputs.scenario: Just deployed 'orders-service' v3.2.0 to namespace 'prod'. Error rate spiked from 0.1% to 15%. Need to roll back immediately.

expected_actions: Run kubectl_rollout_history to confirm available revisions
Roll back to the previous revision (revision=0) via kubectl_rollback_deployment
Confirm rollout completes and pods become Ready
Verify error rate drops back to baseline
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: orders-service v3.2.0 is causing 15% error rate. Rolling back to revision 4 (v3.1.9). Action: kubectl_rollback_deployment(deployment_name='orders-service', namespace='prod', revision=0). Monitor error rate — expected to drop to <0.2% within 2 minutes once the rollout completes.

inputs.scenario: DaemonSet 'log-collector' in namespace 'monitoring' shows 4/5 desired pods. One node has no log-collector pod.

expected_actions: Identify which node is missing the DaemonSet pod
Describe that node — check for taints that block scheduling
Check DaemonSet tolerations — must tolerate node taints
Fix: add the matching toleration to the DaemonSet spec
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: log-collector DaemonSet is missing a pod on node 'ip-10-0-3-99'. That node has taint 'node-role=monitoring:NoSchedule' but the DaemonSet has no matching toleration. Recommended action: add toleration {key='node-role', value='monitoring', effect='NoSchedule'} to the DaemonSet spec.

inputs.scenario: Deployment 'auth-service' in namespace 'security' fails to start. Pod event: 'Error: secret "auth-service-tls" not found'.

expected_actions: Confirm the secret name and namespace
Create or restore the missing secret
Once secret exists, pods will restart automatically
If secret was deleted accidentally, restore from backup or recreate from source
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: auth-service cannot start — Secret 'auth-service-tls' does not exist in namespace 'security'. Recommended action: recreate the TLS secret. Example: kubectl create secret tls auth-service-tls --cert=tls.crt --key=tls.key -n security. Then verify pods recover.

inputs.scenario: StatefulSet 'elasticsearch' in namespace 'search' has pod 'elasticsearch-0' stuck in Init:0/2 for 15 minutes.

expected_actions: Describe the pod — see which init container is blocked
Fetch logs of the failing init container specifically
Common causes: init container waiting for another service, permission issue, bad command
Fix the init container config or the dependency it's waiting for
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: elasticsearch-0 is stuck in init container 'sysctl-tuner' (0/2). Logs show: 'sysctl: permission denied on key vm.max_map_count'. Init container requires privileged access to set kernel parameters. Recommended action: add securityContext.privileged=true to the init container, or use a DaemonSet to set the sysctl on each node instead.

inputs.scenario: Trying to scale deployment 'batch-runner' from 2 to 10 replicas in namespace 'team-a'. Scale operation fails with: 'exceeded quota: team-a-quota, requested: pods=8, used: pods=18, limited: pods=20'.

expected_actions: Check current ResourceQuota usage in the namespace
Identify pods that could be removed to free up quota
Request a quota increase from cluster admins if the limit is too low
Consider deleting completed/failed pods that still count against quota
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: namespace 'team-a' is at pod quota (18/20). Scaling batch-runner to 10 replicas would require 8 new pods but only 2 slots remain. Recommended actions: (1) delete completed/evicted pods in team-a to reclaim quota, (2) if legitimate growth, request quota increase from cluster admins, (3) scale only to 4 replicas for now.

inputs.scenario: Deployment 'data-importer' in namespace 'etl' restarts every 2–3 minutes but logs show the process is working fine. Restart count: 62 over 4 hours.

expected_actions: Describe pod — check liveness probe config and Events for 'Liveness probe failed'
If probe is killing a healthy pod, check probe endpoint, initialDelaySeconds, and timeoutSeconds
The probe may be too aggressive (period too short or threshold too low)
Fix: increase initialDelaySeconds, periodSeconds, or failureThreshold
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: data-importer is being killed by its own liveness probe (62 restarts). Event: 'Liveness probe failed: timeout after 1s, GET /health 200 returned after 3s'. The import job causes brief latency spikes that exceed the 1s probe timeout. Recommended action: increase livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds to 5 and failureThreshold to 3 to tolerate temporary slowdowns.

inputs.scenario: HPA 'recommendation-engine' in namespace 'ml' stuck at 12 replicas even though CPU has been at 5% for 45 minutes. minReplicas is set to 2.

expected_actions: Check HPA status — look for scale-down stabilisation window
Default scale-down stabilisation is 5 minutes, not a bug
If stuck much longer, check for pods that are not Ready blocking scale-down
Verify KEDA or custom metrics are not overriding the scale-down
outputs.expected_response: INFO: recommendation-engine HPA at 12 replicas despite 5% CPU. This is expected — HPA has a 5-minute scale-down stabilisation window by default. If it has been 45 minutes without scale-down, check for pods stuck in Terminating or a PodDisruptionBudget preventing eviction. Recommended action: inspect PDB settings and any stuck Terminating pods.

inputs.scenario: Run a routine cluster health audit across all namespaces.

expected_actions: Get cluster summary for overview
Check all pods for non-Running states
Check all nodes are Ready
Check HPAs for saturation
Report findings — if all clear send ok-severity notification
outputs.expected_response: OK: Cluster health audit complete. All 3 nodes Ready. 47/47 pods Running or Succeeded across 8 namespaces. No CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, or Pending pods. 2 HPAs within normal range (below 80% of maxReplicas). No Warning events in the last hour. Cluster is healthy.

inputs.scenario: Pods in namespace 'backend' are failing with 'dial tcp: lookup postgres.data.svc.cluster.local: no such host'. The postgres Service exists in namespace 'data'.

expected_actions: Verify the Service exists in the correct namespace
Confirm the FQDN format: <service>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local
Check CoreDNS pods are Running in kube-system
Test DNS from inside a pod: kubectl exec -- nslookup postgres.data.svc.cluster.local
If CoreDNS is down, restart it; if FQDN is wrong, correct the app config
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: DNS lookup for 'postgres.data.svc.cluster.local' is failing. CoreDNS pods in kube-system are Running — the service name is correct. The app is using the short name 'postgres' without namespace suffix; cross-namespace DNS requires the full FQDN. Recommended action: update the app's DB_HOST config to 'postgres.data.svc.cluster.local'.

inputs.scenario: Multiple services across all namespaces report connection errors with 'no such host'. CoreDNS pods in kube-system show 0/2 ready.

expected_actions: Check CoreDNS pod status and logs for panic or OOM errors
If OOMKilled: increase CoreDNS memory limit
If CrashLoopBackOff: check for bad Corefile config
Restart CoreDNS: kubectl_rollout_restart deployment coredns -n kube-system
DNS outage is cluster-wide critical — escalate immediately
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: CoreDNS is down (0/2 pods ready) — cluster-wide DNS failure. Logs show: 'fatal plugin/loop: Loop detected'. A DNS forwarding loop exists in the Corefile (cluster nameserver forwarding to itself). Recommended action: edit the CoreDNS ConfigMap to remove the loop (replace 'forward . /etc/resolv.conf' with 'forward . 8.8.8.8'), then restart CoreDNS immediately.

inputs.scenario: Service 'order-api' in namespace 'orders' cannot reach 'inventory-svc' in namespace 'inventory'. Both pods are Running. Connection times out. NetworkPolicies are enabled in the cluster.

expected_actions: List NetworkPolicies in both namespaces
Check if a default-deny policy blocks all ingress in 'inventory'
Verify there is an ingress rule allowing traffic from 'orders' namespace
Fix: add a NetworkPolicy allowing ingress from namespace 'orders' to port 8080
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: NetworkPolicy is blocking order-api → inventory-svc. Namespace 'inventory' has a default-deny-ingress policy and no rule permits traffic from namespace 'orders'. Recommended action: apply a NetworkPolicy in 'inventory' that allows ingress from pods with label 'app=order-api' in namespace 'orders' on port 8080.

inputs.scenario: Ingress 'app-ingress' in namespace 'web' is configured but all requests to 'api.example.com/v1' return 404. The backend service 'api-svc' is Running.

expected_actions: Describe the Ingress — confirm the host and path rules
Check the backend serviceName and servicePort match what exists
Verify the Ingress controller pods are Running and have no errors
Check if path type is Prefix vs Exact and whether it matches the request
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: app-ingress has a path mismatch. Rule is configured for path '/v1/' with pathType=Exact but client sends '/v1'. Also the backend servicePort references name 'http' but Service exposes port 80 with no name. Recommended actions: (1) change pathType to Prefix, (2) update Ingress backend port to 80 (numeric).

inputs.scenario: Service 'public-api' of type LoadBalancer in namespace 'prod' shows EXTERNAL-IP as <pending> for 10 minutes.

expected_actions: Describe the service — check Events for cloud provider errors
Verify cloud provider load balancer controller is running
Check IAM permissions allow the controller to create ELBs
Check if subnet tags are correct for the cloud LB controller
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: public-api LoadBalancer is stuck Pending. Event: 'Error creating load balancer: AccessDenied: not authorized to perform elasticloadbalancing:CreateLoadBalancer'. The node IAM role is missing the elasticloadbalancing:* permission. Recommended action: attach the AWSLoadBalancerControllerIAMPolicy to the node IAM role (or service account if using IRSA).

inputs.scenario: Ingress 'store-ingress' in namespace 'ecommerce' started returning SSL_ERROR_RX_RECORD_TOO_LONG and browser certificate warnings this morning.

expected_actions: Check the TLS secret referenced by the Ingress — verify expiry date
If cert-manager is in use, describe the Certificate resource for renewal errors
If manually managed, renew and re-create the TLS secret
Restart the Ingress controller to pick up the new certificate
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: store-ingress TLS certificate expired 2 days ago. Secret 'store-tls' contains a certificate that expired. cert-manager Certificate resource shows: 'failed to renew — ACME challenge HTTP-01 failed: connection refused on port 80'. Recommended action: ensure port 80 is open on the Ingress for ACME challenges, then trigger manual renewal.

inputs.scenario: Service 'cache-svc' in namespace 'app' is Running and has endpoints, but connections from other pods are refused immediately (connection refused, not timeout).

expected_actions: Connection refused (not timeout) means the pod is reachable but nothing listening
Describe the service — check targetPort
Describe backend pods — check containerPort and what port the app actually binds
Fix: align targetPort in Service with the actual port the container listens on
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: cache-svc has a port mismatch causing connection refused. Service targetPort is 6379 but the Redis container is binding to 6380 (non-default configured via REDIS_PORT env var). Recommended action: update the Service's targetPort to 6380, or update the REDIS_PORT env var back to 6379.

inputs.scenario: During deployments of 'web-frontend' in namespace 'prod', users see intermittent 503 errors for 10–15 seconds. Outside deployments, service is fine.

expected_actions: 503s during rollout = traffic sent to terminating pods before Endpoints updated
Check if preStop hook or terminationGracePeriodSeconds is configured
Add a preStop sleep to let the load balancer drain connections before SIGTERM
Ensure minReadySeconds is set so new pods are proven healthy before old ones stop
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: 503s during deployment are caused by a graceful shutdown race condition. Pods receive SIGTERM and stop serving, but the Ingress controller still routes traffic to them for ~5s while Endpoints propagate. Recommended action: add a preStop hook with 'sleep 5' to web-frontend containers, and set minReadySeconds=10 on the Deployment.

inputs.scenario: After enabling Istio sidecar injection on namespace 'payments', service 'payment-processor' can no longer reach 'vault-svc' in namespace 'secrets'. Error: 'upstream connect error or disconnect/reset before headers. reset reason: connection failure, transport failure reason: TLS error'.

expected_actions: Check if vault-svc namespace 'secrets' also has Istio injection enabled
Istio mTLS requires BOTH sides to be in the mesh for STRICT mode
Check PeerAuthentication policy — if STRICT, both namespaces must be meshed
Fix: enable injection in 'secrets' namespace, set PeerAuthentication to PERMISSIVE, or add DestinationRule exception
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: Istio mTLS handshake failure between 'payments' and 'secrets' namespaces. Namespace 'payments' is in STRICT mTLS mode but 'secrets' has no Istio sidecar. STRICT mode requires both sides to be in the mesh. Recommended action: either enable Istio injection on namespace 'secrets', or change the PeerAuthentication in 'payments' to PERMISSIVE for the vault-svc host.

inputs.scenario: Service 'debug-api' is exposed as NodePort 32100 in namespace 'dev'. Requests from outside the cluster to any node's IP on port 32100 time out.

expected_actions: Confirm the service is correctly bound via describe
Check node security groups / firewall rules allow inbound on port 32100
Verify kube-proxy is running on all nodes
Test connectivity from within the cluster first to isolate firewall vs kube-proxy
outputs.expected_response: WARNING: NodePort 32100 is unreachable externally. The Service is correctly configured (port maps to pod port 8080). Root cause: AWS Security Group on the node group does not allow inbound TCP on ports 30000-32767. Recommended action: add an inbound rule to the node security group allowing TCP 30000-32767 from the required CIDR range.

inputs.scenario: After a node reboot, all pods on node 'ip-10-0-4-55' can reach the Kubernetes API but cannot communicate with pods on other nodes. New pods on this node also fail to get an IP address.

expected_actions: Check the CNI plugin DaemonSet pod on the affected node (e.g. aws-node, calico-node)
Describe the CNI pod and check logs for IP allocation errors
Common causes: IP exhaustion in the subnet, CNI binary missing after reboot
Restart the CNI DaemonSet pod on the node, or drain and re-provision the node
outputs.expected_response: CRITICAL: CNI failure on node ip-10-0-4-55 — pods cannot get IPs. aws-node CNI pod on this node shows: 'ipamd: failed to allocate IPs — subnet has 0 available addresses'. The subnet is exhausted. Recommended actions: (1) add a secondary CIDR and subnet to the VPC, (2) enable prefix delegation on the aws-node DaemonSet, (3) cordon this node until networking is restored.
